Examples
========

This section showcases example C files and their generated VHDL output.
The VHDL snippets below are representative outputs generated from the shown
inputs unless noted otherwise.

The ``examples/`` folder in the repository contains additional input files.

Basic Function
--------------

A simple function with local variable and return value.

**C input** (inline example):

.. code-block:: c

   int add(int a, int b) {
       int sum = a + b;
       return sum;
   }

**VHDL output:**

.. code-block:: vhdl

   -- VHDL generated by gates

   library IEEE;
   use IEEE.STD_LOGIC_1164.ALL;
   use IEEE.NUMERIC_STD.ALL;

   -- Function: add
   entity add is
     port (
       clk   : in  std_logic;
       reset : in  std_logic;
       a : in std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
       b : in std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
       result : out std_logic_vector(31 downto 0)
     );
   end entity;

   architecture behavioral of add is
     signal sum : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
   begin
     process(clk, reset)
     begin
       if reset = '1' then
         sum <= (others => '0');
       elsif rising_edge(clk) then
         sum <= a + b;
         result <= sum;
       end if;
     end process;
   end architecture;

Each C function becomes an entity with clock/reset ports, parameters as
``std_logic_vector`` inputs, and a ``result`` output. Local variables become
signals with zero-initialized reset logic.

Cross-function calls are still a documented limitation: multi-function inputs
emit separate entities, but gates does not yet synthesize the inter-entity
wiring needed to connect them automatically.

Control Flow (if/else)
----------------------

Conditional branching maps to VHDL ``if``/``else`` inside the synchronous process.

**C input:**

.. code-block:: c

   int abs_val(int x) {
       if (x < 0) {
           return 0 - x;
       } else {
           return x;
       }
   }

**VHDL output:**

.. code-block:: vhdl

   -- Function: abs_val
   entity abs_val is
     port (
       clk   : in  std_logic;
       reset : in  std_logic;
       x : in std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
       result : out std_logic_vector(31 downto 0)
     );
   end entity;

   architecture behavioral of abs_val is
   begin
     process(clk, reset)
     begin
       if reset = '1' then
       elsif rising_edge(clk) then
         if unsigned(x) < to_unsigned(0, 64) then
           result <= c_0 - x;
         else
           result <= x;
         end if;
       end if;
     end process;
   end architecture;

For Loop
--------

C ``for`` loops are desugared into initialization, a VHDL ``while`` loop with
the condition, body statements, and an increment assignment.

**C input:**

.. code-block:: c

   int loop_sum(int n) {
       int sum = 0;
       for (int i = 0; i < n; i = i + 1) {
           sum = sum + i;
       }
       return sum;
   }

**VHDL output:**

.. code-block:: vhdl

   -- Function: loop_sum
   entity loop_sum is
     port (
       clk   : in  std_logic;
       reset : in  std_logic;
       n : in std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
       result : out std_logic_vector(31 downto 0)
     );
   end entity;

   architecture behavioral of loop_sum is
     signal sum : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
     signal i : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
   begin
     process(clk, reset)
     begin
       if reset = '1' then
         sum <= (others => '0');
         i <= (others => '0');
       elsif rising_edge(clk) then
         sum <= c_0;
         i <= c_0;
         while unsigned(i) < unsigned(n) loop
           sum <= sum + i;
           i <= i + c_1;
         end loop;
         result <= sum;
       end if;
     end process;
   end architecture;

Note that ``i++`` is desugared to ``i = i + 1`` during parsing, and integer
literals ``0`` and ``1`` are emitted as constants ``c_0`` and ``c_1``.

Structs
-------

C structs become VHDL record types. Struct-typed parameters use the record
type directly. Field access maps to VHDL record field notation.

**C input** (``examples/struct_example.c``):

.. code-block:: c

   struct Point { int x; int y; };

   int add_points(struct Point p) {
       return p.x + p.y;
   }

**VHDL output** (first entity only):

.. code-block:: vhdl

   -- Struct Point as VHDL record
   type Point_t is record
     x : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
     y : std_logic_vector(31 downto 0);
   end record;

   -- Function: add_points
   entity add_points is
     port (
       clk   : in  std_logic;
       reset : in  std_logic;
       p : in Point_t;
       result : out std_logic_vector(31 downto 0)
     );
   end entity;

   architecture behavioral of add_points is
   begin
     process(clk, reset)
     begin
       if reset = '1' then
       elsif rising_edge(clk) then
         result <= p.x + p.y;
       end if;
     end process;
   end architecture;

Running the Examples
--------------------

.. code-block:: bash

   # Compile any C file to VHDL
   ./build/gates examples/example.c output.vhdl

   # View the generated output
   cat output.vhdl
